title: HMSET
description: Sets the values of multiple fields.
complexity: O(N) where N is the number of fields being set.

sections.title: Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility

content:
Sets the specified fields to their respective values in the hash stored at `key`. This command overwrites any specified fields already existing in the hash. If `key` does not exist, a new key holding a hash is created. ## Examples HMSET myhash field1 "Hello" field2 "World" HGET myhash field1 HGET myhash field2 ## Redis Software and Redis Cloud compatibility | Redis
Software | Redis
Cloud | Notes | |:----------------------|:-----------------|:------| | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | ✅ Standard
✅ Active-Active | Deprecated as of Redis v4.0.0. | ## Return information **RESP2:** [Simple string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-strings): `OK`. **RESP3:** [Simple string reply](../../develop/reference/protocol-spec#simple-strings): `OK`.
intro:
Sets the specified fields to their respective values in the hash stored at
`key`.
This command overwrites any specified fields already existing in the hash.
If `key` does not exist, a new key holding a hash is created.

sections.title: Overview
sections.content:
Sets the specified fields to their respective values in the hash stored at
`key`.
This command overwrites any specified fields already existing in the hash.
If `key` does not exist, a new key holding a hash is created.

sections.title: Examples
sections.content:
HMSET myhash field1 "Hello" field2 "World"
HGET myhash field1
HGET myhash field2
